channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Prisons Officers Celebrate New Mothers at Homa Bay Hospital on International Women’s Day
item.description: At Homa Bay Teaching and Referral Hospital, the maternity ward was buzzing with smiles and the sounds of newborns as Kenya Prisons officers, together with members of the judiciary, paid a visit to cel...

item.title: George Russell triumphs in Australian Grand Prix, outpacing Leclerc, while Piastri crashes
item.description: George Russell delivered a commanding performance to win the Australian Grand Prix, kicking off his 2026 Formula 1 world championship campaign in style. Starting from pole, the British driver engaged ...

item.title: Government launches programme to help women secure land ownership
item.description: All over Tanzania, from village footpaths to city streets, land is very important for families to build homes and grow crops. But historically, many women who work the land didn’t have formal ownershi...

item.title: Investigation Underway Into Deadly Attack on Girls’ School in Minab, Iran
item.description: On March 5, 2026, a girls’ elementary school in Minab, Iran, was struck in a deadly attack that resulted in the deaths of at least 175 people, the majority of whom were children. Following the attack,...

item.title: Gwadar fisherman dies after debris from intercepted missile hits boat in Iranian waters
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in the Iranian waters on Saturday when Muhammad Tayyab, a fisherman from Gannz in Gwadar, lost his life. According to Gwadar Deputy Commissioner Naqibullah Kakar, Tayyab’s b...

item.title: Iran to Announce Successor Following Khamenei's Death Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Iran is set to announce a new supreme leader after the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in a recent US-Israeli air strike. The Assembly of Experts, responsible for choosing the successor, has already v...

item.title: Conflict in Iran Causes Widespread Regional Disruption and Global Concerns
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has significantly destabilized much of the Middle East following missile and drone strikes by the U.S. and Israel on February 28, 2022. Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Al...

item.title: Review of Trillium Restaurant in Birmingham: Bold, Playful, and Delightful Dining
item.description: Trillium, the latest venture from Michelin-starred chef Glyn Purnell in Birmingham, offers a dining experience that is both high-quality and unpretentious. Far from a quiet, formal setting, Trillium i...

item.title: Mumbai University Postpones Third-Year Examinations, Announces New Schedule
item.description: The University of Mumbai has officially postponed the third-year examinations for its undergraduate and postgraduate programmes. Students who were preparing for these exams are advised to take note of...

item.title: George Russell secures comfortable victory for Mercedes in 2026 Australian Grand Prix season opener
item.description: George Russell claimed a dominant victory in the Australian Grand Prix, marking the start of Formula 1's new era with updated hybrid power units and overtaking aids. The Mercedes driver led from pole ...

item.title: Pep Guardiola to Miss Two Matches but Will Coach Man City in Carabao Cup Final
item.description: Pep Guardiola, the Spanish coach of Manchester City, has been handed a two-match touchline ban following a yellow card he received during the FA Cup fifth-round clash against Newcastle United, which C...

item.title: Dr. Paul warns that most teenagers are not meeting recommended sleep levels
item.description: A growing number of Canadian teenagers are not achieving the recommended amount of sleep, according to Dr. Paul. Experts emphasize that insufficient sleep in adolescence can lead to a range of health ...

item.title: Top Dawlah Islamiya-Hassan Leader Emarudin Kulaw Killed in Military Clash in Banisilan, Cotabato
item.description: On March 8, 2026, soldiers from the Philippine Army's 6th Infantry Division and 601st and 602nd Infantry Brigades killed Emarudin Kulaw, the known emir or top leader of the Dawlah Islamiya-Hassan fact...

item.title: Piastri crashes in warm-up, forced to miss Australian Grand Prix
item.description: Oscar Piastri faced heartbreak at the Australian Grand Prix after a pre-race spin in the warm-up session ended his race before it even began. The 24-year-old McLaren driver was set to start fifth on t...

item.title: South Africa’s Tourism Industry Shows Steady Growth and Premium Focus
item.description: After the challenges of the Covid pandemic, South Africa’s tourism industry is showing strong signs of recovery and evolution. Across the country, hotels are expanding, international brands are enteri...

item.title: Swedish Students Create the World's Smallest 3D-Printed Beer Bottle
item.description: A team of five students from Sweden's KTH Royal Institute of Technology has broken the record for the world's smallest beer bottle. Using a high-precision 3D printer, the students produced a 4-millime...

item.title: Empowering Women and Girls is Key for Tanzania’s Vision 2050
item.description: If Tanzania wan achieve its Vision 2050 for prosperity, growth and resilience, we cannot ignore the role of women and girls. Demographically and economically, women already dey lead in many areas – fr...

item.title: Nishant Kumar Joins JD(U) Amid Speculations of Deputy CM Role in Bihar
item.description: On 8th March 2026, Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, formally joined the Janata Dal (United) party, shortly after the party chief filed his nomination for a Rajya Sabha seat in ...

item.title: How Nairobi’s weak planning and drainage left residents at risk of deadly floods
item.description: The article looks at how Nairobi ended up suffering deadly floods despite repeated alerts about heavy rains and rising flood risk. It explains that weather and climate experts had issued several early...

item.title: Harry Styles shares his experience coping with the loss of Liam Payne
item.description: British singer Harry Styles recently spoke candidly about the emotional challenges he has faced following the passing of his former One Direction bandmate, Liam Payne. In interviews, Styles described ...
